ddls:
CREATE TABLE "parking_activity" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the activity log */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the activity log */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the activity log */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the activity log */,
  "minute" INTEGER /* Minute of the activity log */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., entrance, exit) */,
  "license_plate" TEXT /* License plate of the vehicle */,
  "lot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parking lot */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the activity */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle (e.g., car, truck, motorcycle) */,
  "parking_spot_id" TEXT /* ID of the parking spot */,
  "duration_minutes" INTEGER /* Duration of parking in minutes (for exit activities) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parking_activity_lot_id FOREIGN KEY ("lot_id") REFERENCES parking_lots ("lot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parking_activity_license_plate FOREIGN KEY ("license_plate") REFERENCES vehicles ("license_plate")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicles" (
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle */,
  "license_plate" TEXT /* License plate number of the vehicle */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle (e.g., car, truck, motorcycle) */,
  "owner_name" TEXT /* Name of the vehicle owner */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information of the vehicle owner */,
  "make" TEXT /* Make of the vehicle */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the vehicle */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the vehicle */,
  "year_of_manufacture" INTEGER /* Year the vehicle was manufactured */,
  "registered_date" TEXT /* Date the vehicle was registered in the system */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the vehicle (e.g., resident, visitor, staff) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parking_lots" (
  "lot_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parking lot */,
  "lot_name" TEXT /* Name of the parking lot */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the parking lot */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Total capacity of the parking lot */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the parking lot */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the parking lot */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the parking lot */,
  "opening_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the parking lot */,
  "fee_structure" TEXT /* Fee structure for parking */,
  "available_spots" INTEGER /* Number of available spots in real-time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login purposes */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, staff) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address for contact */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "device_info" TEXT /* Information about the device used (e.g., browser, OS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "sent_time" TEXT /* Time when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., alert, update, reminder) */,
  "read_status" TEXT /* Read status of the notification (e.g., read, unread) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
